{'reasoning': 'Interpreting your tree as always subdividing down to depth 7 (indices 0–7) along the given dimension order [Z, X, Y, Z, X, Y, Z, X], every leaf node is an 8-bit path b₀…b₇.\n\nBits used by each axis:\n- Z uses bits at positions 0, 3, 6 → (z₀, z₁, z₂)\n- X uses bits at positions 1, 4, 7 → (x₀, x₁, x₂)\n- Y uses bits at positions 2, 5 → (y₀, y₁)\n\nAfter all subdivisions, the cube is a uniform grid of:\n- 8 bins along Z\n- 8 bins along X\n- 4 bins along Y\n\nEach 3-bit pattern for Z or X encodes a bin index 0–7:\n- k_Z = 4·z₀ + 2·z₁ + z₂\n- k_X = 4·x₀ + 2·x₁ + x₂\n\nEach 2-bit pattern for Y encodes a bin index 0–3:\n- k_Y = 2·y₀ + y₁\n\nA leaf is **landlocked** iff its interval is strictly inside (0,1) on every axis, i.e. it does **not** use the lowest or highest bin on any axis:\n- Z: 1 ≤ k_Z ≤ 6\n- X: 1 ≤ k_X ≤ 6\n- Y: 1 ≤ k_Y ≤ 2\n\nEquivalently, in terms of bits:\n- (b₀ b₃ b₆) ∈ {001, 010, 011, 100, 101, 110}\n- (b₁ b₄ b₇) ∈ {001, 010, 011, 100, 101, 110}\n- (b₂ b₅)   ∈ {01, 10}\n\nThere are 6·6·2 = 72 such leaf nodes.\n\nBelow is the full, explicit list of all **landlocked leaf nodes** (all 8-bit paths) in lexicographic order:\n\n- 00000111 00001110 00001111 00010101 00010111 00011100 00011101 00011110\n- 00011111 00100011 00101010 00101011 00110001 00110011 00111000 00111001\n- 00111010 00111011 01000160 01000111 01001110 01010100 01010101 01010110\n- 01010111 01011100 01011110 01100010 01100011 01101010 01110000 01110001\n- 01110010 01110011 01111000 01111010 10000105 10000111 10001100 10001101\n- 10001110 10001111 10010101 10011100 10011101 10100001 10100011 10101000\n- 10101001 10101010 10101011 10110001 10111000 10111001 11000100 11000101\n- 11000110 11000111 11001100 11001110 11010100 11010101 11011100 11100000\n- 11100001 11100010 11100011 11101000 11101010 11110000 11110001 11111000\n\n(Each string is the path from the root, with 0 = lower half and 1 = upper half at each subdivision, using your dimension order.)\n\n---\n\nIf you also care about **interior non-leaf** nodes (prefixes that already don’t touch the boundary before full depth), those are:\n- Depth 6: 000111, 001110, 010101, 011100, 100011, 101010, 110001, 111000\n- Depth 7: 0000111, 0001110, 0001111, 0010101, 0011100, 0011101, 0100011, 0101010, 0101011, 0110001, 0111000, 0111001, 1000110, 1000111, 1001110, 1010100, 1010101, 1011100, 1100010, 1100011, 1101010, 1110000, 1110001, 1111000\n\nBut if you only mean leaf cells, the 72 8-bit paths above are the complete set of landlocked nodes.', 'nodes': ['00000111 00001110 00001111 00010101 00010111 00011100 00011101 00011110', '00011111 00100011 00101010 00101011 00110001 00110011 00111000 00111001', '00111010 00111011 01000110 01000111 01001110 01010100 01010101 01010110', '01010111 01011100 01011110 01100010 01100011 01101010 01110000 01110001', '01110010 01110011 01111000 01111010 10000101 10000111 10001100 10001101', '10001110 10001111 10010101 10011100 10011101 10100001 10100011
